Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

> How is this different from https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/e2e/artifact_test.go? Testing the same functionality twice is a waste of resources.

We don't run those e2e tests yet. I think there are lots of duplication anyway between the e2e and the BATS tests.

> I think there are lots of duplication anyway between the e2e and the BATS tests.

There certainly are duplicates. However that is not a reason to add more.

Duplicating tests means we waste time. The time it takes people to write and review tests that are already there. Then you get to deal with double the changes if you ever have to modify the test due code changes and so on. To me it is a clear waste of maintenance effort. And then the obvious issue of CI time is slower because we test the same thing several times.

> We don't run those e2e tests yet.

Sure but fixing that seems to be the much better over rewriting e2e tests to system tests.

> > We don't run those e2e tests yet.
> 
> Sure but fixing that seems to be the much better over rewriting e2e tests to system tests.

Another issue is that the `podman-tests` package in Fedora only ships BATS tests and the `podman-testing` binary.

I just ran the e2e tests with `PODMAN_BINARY=/usr/bin/podman QUADLET_BINARY=/usr/libexec/podman/quadlet make localintegration` and got:

```
....
  [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact remove
  /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:167
  [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact extract single
  /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:239
  [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact simple add --append
  /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:407
  [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact add --append modified file
  /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:473
  [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact add --append file already exists in artifact
  /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:505

Ran 2149 of 2329 Specs in 510.063 seconds
FAIL! -- 2089 Passed | 60 Failed | 0 Pending | 180 Skipped
```

> I just ran the e2e tests with `PODMAN_BINARY=/usr/bin/podman QUADLET_BINARY=/usr/libexec/podman/quadlet make localintegration` and got:
> 
> ```
> ....
>   [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact remove
>   /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:167
>   [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact extract single
>   /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:239
>   [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact simple add --append
>   /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:407
>   [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact add --append modified file
>   /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:473
>   [FAIL] Podman artifact [It] podman artifact add --append file already exists in artifact
>   /home/ricardo/podman/test/e2e/artifact_test.go:505
> 
> Ran 2149 of 2329 Specs in 510.063 seconds
> FAIL! -- 2089 Passed | 60 Failed | 0 Pending | 180 Skipped
> ```

Well yeah tests are tested against the current version of the code not whatever version you have installed in /usr/bin/podman. Many podman artifact work has been added after 5.4.0.
So you need to check out the sources to the 5.4.0 tag to run the tests against a 5.4.0 binary. Or you build podman from main.

> Well yeah tests are tested against the current version of the code not whatever version you have installed in /usr/bin/podman. Many podman artifact work has been added after 5.4.0. So you need to check out the sources to the 5.4.0 tag to run the tests against a 5.4.0 binary. Or you build podman from main.

True.  I just checked `v5.4.0` and the artifact tests pass (some other tests failing, but an improvement nonetheless).  But the output of the Ginkgo tests is more difficult to consume than the TAP output used by BATS.

Are BATS tests deprecated?  I think the documentation on contributing new tests should make it clear.

> Are BATS tests deprecated? I think the documentation on contributing new tests should make it clear.

No not all, there is (for most parts) no right or wrong when choosing between system or e2e so it is whatever the author felt like. There is no policy or guidance around that and I am sure most maintainers here considers things differently.
